\section{Related Work}
\label{sec:related_work}

% Related Work 写作逻辑：
% Review A-Novelty/B-Q1/C-Q2 回应位置：Related Work 集中比较 SQLGlot/jOOQ/RISE/CrackSQL/QTRAN/Sedar，补足 Introduction 中的简短提名。
% 回应逻辑：先把相关工作分成三类，再分别介绍代表工作，最后强调 Morph 的不同。
% - DBMS Fuzzing：现有 generation/mutation/oracle fuzzers 解决如何生成或变异 query，但不解决 emerging DBMS 的 high-quality seed acquisition。
% - LLM-Based Seed Generation：LLM 可用于直接合成、片段合成或 test-case transfer（Fuzz4All/ShQveL/Sedar），但常依赖文本生成和后处理 sanitizer；Morph 把 LLM 限制在 AQT-guided instantiation。
% - Cross-Dialect SQL Translation：SQLGlot/jOOQ/CrackSQL/RISE/QTRAN 等面向 statement/query migration 或 oracle portability，不建模 fuzzing seed 的 test-case-level dependency 和 fuzzer compatibility。
% - Review A-Novelty(vs RISE) 回应位置：本节末尾用 scope（single SELECT vs ordered multi-statement，含 Δ 和 schema context D）与 representation（statement-local rewrite rule vs AQT five-tuple 的局部 instantiation operator）两点区分 AQT 与 RISE 的 query-reduction skeleton。
% - Related Work 不复述 RQ 数字，只保留 conceptual difference。

% Related work falls into three categories: 
% The first category improves how DBMS inputs are generated, mutated, or checked; the second uses LLMs to synthesize or transfer testing inputs; and the third translates SQL across dialects.
% \tool{} builds on these lines of work but targets a different problem: structure-preserving whole-test-case SQL transfer for fuzzing.

\subsection{DBMS Fuzzing}
Fuzzing is one of the most effective automated testing techniques for identifying DBMS vulnerabilities.
Generation-based tools, such as SQLsmith~\cite{sqlsmith}, SQLancer~\cite{sqlancer, rigger2020testing}, and DynSQL~\cite{jiang2023dynsql}, construct SQL queries from hard-coded rules or semantic oracles, while recent clause-guided and differential methods further improve logic-bug detection~\cite{wei2024sqlaser, ba2023testing, song2023testing, cui2022differentially, hao2023pinolo}. However, predefined templates can limit complex scenarios~\cite{gao2023comprehensive}. Mutation-based fuzzers instead evolve seed pools: \squirrel{}~\cite{zhong2020squirrel} uses IR-based mutations, \griffin{}~\cite{fu2022griffin} uses grammar-free metadata replacement, and LEGO~\cite{liang2023sequence} combines SQL statement sequences. 
These fuzzers are effective once suitable seeds or generation rules are available, but they do not directly address how to obtain high-quality seeds for emerging DBMSs.
Unlike these works, \tool{} focuses on the upstream \textit{seed acquisition} problem by transferring mature DBMS test cases into target-compatible seed sequences.

\subsection{LLM-Based Seed Generation}
Recent work has explored LLMs for generating or enriching testing inputs.
Fuzz4All~\cite{xia2024fuzz4all} uses LLMs as a general input generator, and ShQveL~\cite{zhong2025testing} synthesizes SQL fragments for database testing.
\sedar{}~\cite{fu2024sedar} is the closest work to \tool{} because it also transfers existing DBMS test cases into fuzzing seeds.
However, these methods largely rely on direct text generation or post-generation filtering and sanitization.
In contrast, \tool{} performs AQT-guided whole-test-case instantiation, where explicit schema dependencies, nested structures, and clause-level relationships support local repair while preserving context.

\subsection{Cross-Dialect SQL Translation}
General-purpose SQL dialect translators, such as SQLGlot~\cite{sqlglot} and jOOQ~\cite{jooq}, exploit the deterministic structure of SQL dialects and encode dialect mappings as parser or transpiler rules, making them useful for recurring statement-level conversions in query migration and application compatibility.
However, constructing and maintaining these rules requires significant dialect expertise.
Recent studies have reduced the manual effort required for dialect migration.
CrackSQL~\cite{zhou2025cracking} studies LLM-based dialect translation, RISE~\cite{xie2026rise} derives rules through dialect-aware query reduction, and QTRAN~\cite{lin2025qtran} extends metamorphic-oracle based testing across DBMS dialects.

Among these translation-oriented systems, RISE~\cite{xie2026rise} is the closest comparison, because its dialect-aware query reduction also separates dialect-specific elements from surrounding SQL.
It translates a query by first reducing a complex input to a simpler form, learning the local dialect rewrite from that reduced form, and then applying the rewrite back to the original query.
However, RISE still treats a single query as the translation unit.
The final DQL statement within a source seed often relies on schema elements and data conditions created by prior DDL and DML statements. Translating the query in isolation does not produce a valid seed if those preparatory statements are not migrated in a consistent manner.
Unlike RISE, which transfers a reduced query containing a single dialect element, \tool{} transfers the entire seed sequence as normalized per-statement forms within an AQT while preserving cross-statement dependencies and evolving schema context.

Overall, existing SQL translation systems do not target fuzzing-oriented whole-test-case seed transfer, where cross-statement dependencies, evolving schema context, and compatibility with downstream fuzzer grammars must be preserved.

\section{Conclusion}
\label{sec:conclusion}
% Conclusion 写作逻辑：
% - 一句话回收 problem/method/results/future work，不再引入新概念。
% - 保持和全文术语一致：Dialect-Aware AQT Instantiation + adaptive sanitization。
We propose \tool{}, an automated fuzzing framework that extracts dialect-agnostic AQTs, instantiates them through Dialect-Aware AQT Instantiation, and applies adaptive sanitization for cross-dialect DBMS fuzzing. Across four DBMSs, \tool{} improves semantic correctness by up to 272\%, achieves higher coverage than \sedar{}, and detects \bugcnt{} unknown bugs. Future work will extend \tool{} beyond relational DBMSs.
